summary: Read-first, SQLi-safe admin DB console over Postgres. Ported from reference-platform dbadmin.go patterns per docs/brain2daax.md §3 (F6), §10 (D4).

F6 depends on F5 (#101, RBAC tables rbac_users/rbac_roles/rbac_user_roles + auth_audit) which is OPEN/not built. Built F6 table-agnostic + forward-compatible: it inspects ALL non-system base tables via information_schema, so RBAC tables auto-appear when F5 lands. No RBAC migrations added here (F5 owns those) to avoid collision.

Table/column identifiers are NEVER interpolated from input. Each is validated against information_schema (must exist as a public BASE TABLE / column of that table) then quoted with pg.escapeIdentifier (TS equivalent of pgx.Identifier.Sanitize). Values are always bound as parameters cast to the column's catalog type ($N::udt_name). An injected/non-existent identifier is rejected before any query executes.

requireSuperAdmin is env-driven (DAAX_DB_CONSOLE_SUPERADMINS, comma list of usernames/emails), disjoint from the RBAC tables it can read, so DB access cannot be self-escalated through those tables. Default-DENY when the allow-list is empty (fail closed). Console routes call requireAuth() then requireSuperAdmin(). Host-dev: set DAAX_DB_CONSOLE_SUPERADMINS=local (the LOCAL_OPERATOR username).

Writes OFF by default behind DAAX_DB_CONSOLE_WRITES=1. When enabled, a write to an audited table (DAAX_DB_CONSOLE_AUDITED_TABLES, default rbac_users,rbac_roles,rbac_user_roles) MUST force an auth_audit row in the SAME transaction; if auth_audit is absent or cannot be populated, the write is REFUSED (fail-closed). UPDATE/DELETE require a non-empty WHERE (no unconditional mass mutation). auth_audit columns are introspected (forward-compatible with F5's eventual schema).

Producer: Claude (Anthropic). Validator: Codex/GPT (OpenAI) pre-PR gate. Test harness: tests/lib/db-console.test.ts (unit: identifier/SQLi rejection, param binding, super-admin gate, write-flag) + tests/integration/db-console-pg.test.ts (real Postgres: list/inspect, adversarial injection neutralized, write-off refused, write-on + audit row, audit-missing fail-closed).
